homepage.heroSubline: Custom solutions for the Central Valley's unique climate. Serving Elk Grove, Roseville, and Folsom with water-smart systems designed for our clay soils and extreme summers.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Onyx Irrigation grew from a single truck serving Midtown bungalows to a full team maintaining systems across Sacramento County. We know how 100°F summers warp PVC pipes and how winter rains test drainage solutions.

Our approach combines drought-resistant design principles with practical fixes for older neighborhoods. Whether it's fixing 1940s bungalow sprinklers or installing smart controllers in Natomas developments, we adapt to your property's needs.
We sponsor the Sacramento Tree Foundation's planting initiatives and offer free irrigation checkups during regional water conservation weeks.

valueProps.title: Clay Soil Specialists
valueProps.description: Our systems account for Sacramento's expansive clay soils that crack pipes and shift sprinkler heads.

valueProps.description: Expert repairs for the unique irrigation challenges of Midtown's historic properties.

valueProps.title: New Development Ready
valueProps.description: Full system installations tailored to HOA requirements in master-planned communities.

homepage.ctaSubline: Get a free assessment of your system's water efficiency before summer's peak heat arrives.

services.name: Smart Irrigation Installation
services.shortDescription: Upgrade to weather-responsive controllers that automatically adjust watering based on Sacramento's microclimates.
services.description:
Sacramento's temperature swings between Midtown and Folsom demand smart irrigation solutions. We install Wi-Fi enabled controllers from Hunter and Rain Bird that sync with local weather data, reducing water waste by up to 50%. 

Systems include flow sensors that detect leaks in real-time - critical for preventing clay soil erosion under 100°F heat. Our team programs zones to account for shade patterns in Land Park's mature trees versus full sun exposure in newer Elk Grove developments. 

Optional soil moisture sensors add another layer of precision, particularly helpful for rose gardens in East Sacramento or drought-tolerant landscaping in Roseville.
process: Step 1: Property assessment of sun exposure, soil type, and plant water needs
Step 2: Controller selection based on yard size and Wi-Fi coverage
Step 3: Professional installation with concealed wiring where possible
Step 4: Training on mobile app controls and seasonal adjustment reminders
benefits: 30-50% water savings compared to traditional timers
Remote control via smartphone when traveling
Automatic rain delay function during winter storms
Compatible with most existing sprinkler systems

faq.question: Will this work with my 1970s irrigation system?
faq.answer: Yes, we can retrofit smart controllers to most existing systems. The only exceptions are very old mechanical clock timers, which we'll replace with modern components during installation.

faq.question: How does the system handle Sacramento's hard water?
faq.answer: Controllers include self-flushing modes to prevent mineral buildup. We also install inline filters for properties with particularly hard water common in Arden-Arcade areas.

services.name: Drip Irrigation Systems
services.shortDescription: Targeted watering for drought-tolerant landscapes and vegetable gardens, minimizing evaporation loss.
services.description:
Perfect for Sacramento's Mediterranean climate, our drip systems deliver water directly to plant roots with 90% efficiency. We design different zones for native plants versus edible gardens - accounting for the Valley Oak Charter School's demonstration garden needs versus backyard tomatoes in Curtis Park. 

Using pressure-compensating emitters prevents uneven watering during peak summer demand when municipal pressure fluctuates. For sloped properties common in Fair Oaks, we install anti-drain valves to prevent dry spots. 

All tubing is UV-resistant to withstand our intense sunlight, with buried mainlines to protect against gopher damage prevalent in Carmichael soils. Winterization includes blowing out lines to prevent freezing damage during rare cold snaps.
process: Step 1: Mapping plant groupings and water requirements
Step 2: Calculating optimal emitter placement and flow rates
Step 3: Installing color-coded tubing for easy maintenance
Step 4: Pressure testing and mulch integration
benefits: Reduces weed growth by avoiding surface watering
Prevents fungal diseases common in humid microclimates

faq.question: Can drip irrigation work with my existing sprinklers?
faq.answer: We often create hybrid systems, keeping spray heads for lawns while converting planting beds to drip. This approach works well for Tudor-style homes in College Greens with mixed landscaping.

faq.question: How often should the system be flushed?
faq.answer: We recommend annual flushing before summer peak season, included in our maintenance plans. Homes near the American River with silt-heavy water may need semi-annual service.

services.name: Sprinkler System Repair
services.shortDescription: Fast fixes for common Sacramento irrigation issues from broken heads to valve leaks.
services.description:
Sacramento's soil movement and hard water create unique repair challenges we address daily. For Land Park homes with vintage systems, we keep hard-to-find parts for 1980s Toro gear drives. In newer Elk Grove developments, we fix common issues from installer shortcuts like shallow pipes vulnerable to heat warping. 

Our repair process starts with pressure testing to identify leaks in clay soil that can hide for months. We replace broken risers with heavy-duty models that withstand lawn mower impacts common in rental properties. For commercial accounts like McKinley Park's landscaping, we offer after-hours emergency repairs to prevent water waste. 

Winter visits include blowing out systems to prevent freeze damage - especially important for vacation homes in Granite Bay that may sit empty during cold spells.
process: Step 1: Diagnostic testing of all zones
Step 2: Identification of pressure issues or leaks
Step 3: Repair or replacement of damaged components
Step 4: Adjustment of spray patterns to prevent overspray
benefits: Transparent pricing with no trip fees

faq.question: Why do my sprinklers keep getting stuck?
faq.answer: Mineral buildup from our hard water is the usual culprit. We install in-line filters and recommend annual cleanings - particularly important for homes built before 2000 with galvanized pipes.

faq.question: Can you repair systems without digging up my lawn?
faq.answer: Yes, we use trenchless repair methods where possible. For major leaks under driveways in Arden-Arcade's hilly properties, we sometimes recommend rerouting lines to more accessible locations.

services.name: Backflow Prevention Services
services.shortDescription: Certified testing and installation to protect Sacramento's drinking water supply.
services.description:
California law requires annual backflow testing for all irrigation systems - a service we provide with same-day reporting to Sacramento County. Our certified technicians carry all common repair parts for RPZ and PVB devices, crucial for older homes in Boulevard Park where original assemblies may be outdated. 

For new installations, we size devices appropriately based on property water pressure, which varies significantly between high-elevation areas like Sierra Oaks and low-lying Florin Road neighborhoods. All work meets Sacramento City cross-connection control requirements. 

We handle the entire permitting process for commercial properties, including multi-family units in West Sacramento that face stricter compliance deadlines. Emergency replacement services available when devices fail mandatory testing.
process: Step 1: Visual inspection of existing assembly
Step 2: Pressure differential testing
Step 3: Component repair or full replacement
Step 4: Submission of certification paperwork

faq.question: How often must my backflow device be tested?
faq.answer: Sacramento County requires annual testing by June 30th. Commercial properties and multi-family dwellings often have quarterly requirements.

faq.question: Why did my device fail testing?
faq.answer: Common causes include worn seals from our hard water or internal damage from freezing temperatures. Devices older than 15 years often need complete replacement.

services.name: Custom Irrigation Design
services.shortDescription: Tailored water management plans for Sacramento's diverse properties.
services.description:
From historic Capitol Avenue mansions to new construction in Folsom Ranch, we create irrigation blueprints that respect each property's unique needs. Our designs account for Sacramento's 18 distinct soil types - we've mapped clay concentrations from Citrus Heights to the Delta's sandy patches. 

For estates in River Park, we incorporate separate zones for heritage rose gardens versus lawn areas. In Rocklin's newer subdivisions, we design systems that meet strict HOA water-use requirements while keeping turf healthy. All plans include detailed valve maps - invaluable when future repairs are needed behind dense landscaping. 

We coordinate with landscape architects on major renovations, particularly important for homes near the American River where erosion control is critical. CAD drawings available for permit submissions in Sacramento's flood-prone zones.
process: Step 1: Topographic survey and soil analysis
Step 2: Water source evaluation (meter size, pressure)
Step 3: Plant water requirement calculations
Step 4: Hydraulic schematic creation

faq.question: Do I need a new design if I'm remodeling my landscape?
faq.answer: Most major changes require adjustments - we can modify existing plans or create new ones. This is especially important when adding hardscapes common in Midtown courtyard renovations.

faq.question: How do you account for Sacramento's water restrictions?
faq.answer: All designs incorporate current SMUD guidelines and allow for easy adjustment as regulations change. We'll future-proof your system with smart controller compatibility.

reviews.name: Marta K.
reviews.text: The team installed a Rachio controller at our East Sacramento home and completely reconfigured the zones for our mature camellias. When we had that unusual April heat wave, the system automatically increased watering just enough to prevent stress without wasting water. They even relocated a sprinkler head that was hitting our historic brick walkway.

reviews.name: James R.
reviews.text: Found Onyx after three other companies couldn't fix our chronic low pressure in Arden-Arcade. Turns out we had a collapsed pipe under the driveway from soil shifting. They rerouted the line through the side yard without damaging any landscaping and reinforced the remaining pipes. System works better than when it was new.

reviews.name: Lisa M.
reviews.text: Our Curtis Park community garden was wasting so much water until Onyx designed a custom drip system. They created separate zones for our citrus trees versus vegetable beds and included a filter for the sandy loam soil. First year our water bill dropped 30% while plant health improved dramatically.

reviews.name: Carlos T.
reviews.text: Fast response when our apartment complex in West Sac failed inspection. They replaced two RPZ devices same-day and handled all the paperwork with the city. Appreciate that they came back a week later to double-check everything at no charge when we had a pressure fluctuation.

reviews.name: Sarah L.
reviews.text: Working with Onyx on our new build in Folsom Ranch was a game-changer. Their design accounted for the weirdly shaped lot and different sun exposures. The zone layout makes so much sense - no more having to manually water certain areas like our last house. Love the detailed valve map they provided too.

reviews.name: David P.
reviews.text: Fixed our vintage system in Midtown that four other companies said needed full replacement. Found original Toro parts and rebuilt the valves rather than forcing an expensive upgrade. Now our 1930s bungalow's irrigation works perfectly while maintaining its historic character.

service-areas.title: Serving Sacramento County | Onyx Irrigation Areas
service-areas.description: Local irrigation services available in Elk Grove, Roseville, Folsom and throughout Sacramento County's unique microclimates.

serviceAreas.content: Onyx Irrigation provides expert irrigation solutions for Elk Grove's thriving master-planned communities. With hot, dry summers often exceeding 100°F, maintaining lush landscapes requires efficient, water-smart systems. Our team specializes in modern irrigation installations and smart controller upgrades tailored for newer homes (1990s-2020s). We help Elk Grove homeowners conserve water while keeping lawns and gardens vibrant through the intense Sacramento Valley heat.
serviceAreas.localContext: Elk Grove features master-planned neighborhoods with a focus on community amenities and green spaces.

serviceAreas.content: Roseville's planned communities deserve irrigation systems as refined as their neighborhoods. Onyx Irrigation designs drought-resistant solutions for this fast-growing city where summer temperatures regularly top 100°F. From drip irrigation for water-wise landscaping to repairing sprinkler systems in established 1990s-2000s developments, we combine local expertise with water conservation technology. Our services help Roseville residents maintain curb appeal while complying with Placer County water regulations.
serviceAreas.localContext: Roseville blends suburban living with extensive parks and trail systems.

serviceAreas.content: Folsom's newer planned communities benefit from Onyx Irrigation's specialized approach to landscape watering. Whether serving properties near Lake Natoma or hillside developments, we help homeowners protect their investment with optimal watering strategies.
